key_insight: A PINN-based, solver-agnostic training framework can learn turbulence closures that generalize across bluff-body shapes without embedding a CFD solver in the optimization loop.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster screening and deployment of turbulence models for practical CFD, with improved stability and transferability across geometries relevant to fluid-structure and wake engineering.
method: Train closures by imposing the RANS residual with automatic differentiation inside a PINN.
Compare four closure forms: three realizable Reynolds-stress models and one direct force model.
Use input-gradient smoothing and a Lipschitz constraint to improve coupled stability before freezing the closure in a finite-element solver.
evidence_signal: The models were trained on six 2D bluff-body wakes at Re = 10^4 and evaluated with an in-sample and strict leave-one-shape-out protocol; the force model achieved the best out-of-sample mean-velocity and drag performance, with LOSO drag error around 8.5%.
watch_item: Performance still depends on stable deployment in a downstream solver, and the best generalization came from a force model that may be less physically interpretable than stress-based closures.
paper_title: Generalizable turbulence closures across bluff-body shapes by PINN-based solver-agnostic training
